want: start a second workflow without cancelling the first
so_that: I can work on multiple tasks (bugs, features) concurrently without losing progress

want: each workflow's state stored in its own file
so_that: workflows cannot interfere with each other's phase progress, escalations, or skill logs

want: see a list of all active workflows when I start a session
so_that: I can pick which workflow to resume without memorizing branch names or workflow IDs

want: hooks to automatically scope to the correct workflow based on my git branch
so_that: I don't have to manually configure which workflow is active after switching branches

want: my existing workflow state automatically migrated when I upgrade the framework
so_that: I don't lose my in-progress workflow when the state structure changes

want: parallel workflows to work with git worktrees on separate laptops
so_that: I can dogfood the framework across 2 machines working on different backlog items simultaneously

want: to resolve the active workflow ID from the current git branch in a single subprocess call
so_that: all downstream hooks receive consistent, correctly-scoped state without repeated I/O

want: project-level state (counters, constitution, workflow_history) to remain in the root state.json
so_that: project-wide data is accessible regardless of which workflow is active

want: per-workflow state to work within monorepo per-project directories
so_that: each project in my monorepo can have its own independent parallel workflows

want: the framework to handle corrupted state files gracefully
so_that: a single corrupted workflow state doesn't block my entire development session
